verdict.label: Worth checking
verdict.headline: Cantinho da Baia Lunch
verdict.reason: Cantinho da Baia is worth a quick check for lunch, but it is not a guaranteed session.  Treat it as a check-first call rather than a sure thing.  The wind should be manageable, but not a big advantage.  The tide is a bit awkward for the bay, so stay cautious.

conversationalSummary.text: For TODAY, I’d keep it a check-first type of session. Cantinho da Baia at lunch is the best available option, but with the low rising tide and that longer-period stuff it can end up basically flat or too weak in the sheltered bay. I’d check the Surfline cams and the MEO Beachcam before heading out, and only go if it looks meaningfully better than that. If the cams don’t show you some proper, take-offable waves, I’d skip.

photo.alt: Photo of Cantinho da Baia surf spot

wetsuit.reason: 4/3 remains the safer default for Peniche unless water is warm and wind is light.

best.explanation: Cantinho da Baia looks like a fun-sized window for Safe fun, Medium confidence: 0.6-0.9 m, 104 kJ, 8 s period, consistent 78/100, and wind is not fully clear.  A forgiving sandy bay with lots of take-off points; this is best for easy wave count and cleaner faces when the north wind is bothering exposed beaches; 6-9s at low tide is basically flat or too weak in the sheltered bay.  Watch: 6-9s at low tide is basically flat or too weak in the sheltered bay.
best.shortExplanation: Cantinho da Baia is worth a quick check for lunch, but it is not a guaranteed session.  Treat it as a check-first call rather than a sure thing.  The wind should be manageable, but not a big advantage.  The tide is a bit awkward for the bay, so stay cautious.
warnings: 6-9s at low tide is basically flat or too weak in the sheltered bay

ranked.explanation: Lagide looks like a fun-sized window for Fun progression, Medium confidence: 0.9-1.2 m, 144 kJ, 8 s period, consistent 74/100, and wind is the main risk.  A dependable left-hand reef that turns modest swell into real rides; this is best for clean, playful walls when the bigger beaches are too wild or too small; 6-9s at low tide is too shallow and dangerous over the reef shelves.  Watch: wind angle is poor for this spot; 6-9s at low tide is too shallow and dangerous over the reef shelves.
ranked.shortExplanation: Lagide is worth a quick check for lunch, but it is not a guaranteed session.  Treat it as a check-first call rather than a sure thing.  Wind is the main thing to watch.  The tide makes the reef a bit less forgiving.
warnings: wind angle is poor for this spot
6-9s at low tide is too shallow and dangerous over the reef shelves

ranked.explanation: Meio da Baia looks like a fun-sized window for Worth checking, Medium confidence: 0.6-1.1 m, 144 kJ, 8 s period, consistent 75/100, and wind is the main risk.  An inconsistent Baía spot that can work well when north-northwest swell reaches the bay; this is best for bodyboard sessions when the bay is clean, there is enough period and size, and the forecast shows a defined wave; 6-9s at low tide can still make exposed beaches like Belgas / Almagreira / Cerro / Meio da Baia punchy.  Watch: wind angle is poor for this spot.
ranked.shortExplanation: Meio da Baia is worth a quick check for lunch, but it is not a guaranteed session.  Treat it as a check-first call rather than a sure thing.  Wind is the main thing to watch.  The tide and timing are lining up well for this spot.
warnings: wind angle is poor for this spot

ranked.explanation: Cerro looks like a fun-sized window for Worth checking, Medium confidence: 0.6-1.1 m, 110 kJ, 8 s period, consistent 75/100, and wind is the main risk.  A sheltered beachbreak with gentle lefts and rights, somewhat similar to Cantinho but less consistent; this is best for manageable bodyboard waves when north-northwest swell reaches the bay with clean southerly wind; 6-9s at low tide can still make exposed beaches like Belgas / Almagreira / Cerro / Meio da Baia punchy.  Watch: wind angle is poor for this spot.
